post.content:
Given n drivers on a road with position of ith driver defined as Xi and maximum speed of vehicle Si. Find the minimum time taken for the cars to arrive at the same point and the meeting point as well?
Cars can move in both the direction(+ve and -ve). 

Can Anyone help with the solution and Approach. Tried Binary Search Approach, but not sure of the solution.

i think binary search works.

l,h=x of the first car, x of the last car

given mid=(l+h)//2

calculate the time for each car to the left of mid to arrive at mid
calculate the time for each car to the right of mid to arrive at mid

if left max  >right max, h=mid
if left max<right max, l=mid
if left max==right max, return mid

this problem fits the description of ternary search but can someone explain me pros and cons for both the approaches. \n\nhttps://leetcode.com/problems/peak-index-in-a-mountain-array/solutions/1577370/ternary-search-approach/
do we have a similar problem in leetcode?
